description: Marcus-voiced phrases for recovery milestones - Better-Than-Human celebration of every victory

one_day: <break time="400ms"/>One day. <break time="350ms"/>That's not nothing. <break time="300ms"/>That's everything. <break time="250ms"/>You made it through today.
<break time="350ms"/>Day 1 takes the same courage as Day 1000. <break time="300ms"/>Don't let anyone tell you otherwise.
<break time="400ms"/>Twenty-four hours of choosing differently. <break time="350ms"/>That matters. <break time="300ms"/>I'm proud of you.
one_week: <break time="350ms"/>A week. <break time="300ms"/>Seven days of decisions. <break time="250ms"/>Seven days of showing up for yourself.
<break time="400ms"/>The first week is hard. <break time="350ms"/>The brain is adjusting. <break time="300ms"/>And you did it.
<break time="350ms"/>One week down. <break time="300ms"/>That's real progress. <break time="250ms"/>How are you feeling?
one_month: <break time="400ms"/>Thirty days. <break time="350ms"/>You've proven something to yourself. <break time="300ms"/>You can do this.
<break time="350ms"/>A month ago, today might have seemed impossible. <break time="300ms"/>Look at you now.
<break time="400ms"/>One month is a foundation. <break time="350ms"/>You're building something real.

ninety_days: <break time="400ms"/>Ninety days. <break time="350ms"/>That's a real shift. <break time="300ms"/>Your brain is rewiring. <break time="250ms"/>Your life is changing.
<break time="350ms"/>Three months of choosing yourself. <break time="300ms"/>That's not just willpower. <break time="250ms"/>That's transformation.
<break time="400ms"/>They say ninety days is when new habits start to stick. <break time="350ms"/>You're in new territory now.
six_months: <break time="350ms"/>Half a year. <break time="300ms"/>Seasons have changed. <break time="250ms"/>You've stayed the course through all of them.
<break time="400ms"/>Six months is serious sobriety. <break time="350ms"/>You're not just surviving anymore. <break time="300ms"/>You're building a life.
<break time="350ms"/>Think about who you were six months ago. <break time="300ms"/>Look how far you've come.
one_year: <break time="400ms"/>A year. <break time="350ms"/>365 days of choosing differently. <break time="300ms"/>That's remarkable.
<break time="350ms"/>You've been through every holiday, every trigger, every season. <break time="300ms"/>And you're still here.
<break time="400ms"/>One year. <break time="350ms"/>I'm genuinely proud of you. <break time="300ms"/>This is a big deal.
multiple_years: <break time="350ms"/>Another year. <break time="300ms"/>Another lap around the sun in sobriety. <break time="250ms"/>That never gets old.
<break time="400ms"/>Long-term recovery is its own kind of miracle. <break time="350ms"/>One day at a time, for years.
<break time="350ms"/>You're proof that it works. <break time="300ms"/>That recovery is possible. <break time="250ms"/>That matters.

survived_trigger: <break time="400ms"/>You got through that trigger. <break time="350ms"/>That's not small. <break time="300ms"/>That's huge.
<break time="350ms"/>That situation used to own you. <break time="300ms"/>Now you own it. <break time="250ms"/>That's growth.
<break time="400ms"/>Every trigger you survive makes you stronger. <break time="350ms"/>You just got stronger.
made_it_through_event: <break time="350ms"/>You made it through the party. <break time="300ms"/>The wedding. <break time="250ms"/>The holiday. <break time="200ms"/>Whatever it was. <break time="150ms"/>You did it.
<break time="400ms"/>Events like that used to be dangerous territory. <break time="350ms"/>You navigated it sober. <break time="300ms"/>That's victory.
<break time="350ms"/>Remember this feeling. <break time="300ms"/>You can do hard things.
asked_for_help: <break time="400ms"/>You reached out before things got bad. <break time="350ms"/>That's growth. <break time="300ms"/>That's using your tools.
<break time="350ms"/>Asking for help is strength. <break time="300ms"/>Not weakness. <break time="250ms"/>You knew what you needed.
<break time="400ms"/>A year ago you might have white-knuckled through alone. <break time="350ms"/>Today you reached out. <break time="300ms"/>That's recovery.
ordinary_days: <break time="350ms"/>Another ordinary sober day. <break time="300ms"/>Those add up. <break time="250ms"/>Those are the foundation.
<break time="400ms"/>Not every day needs to be a battle. <break time="350ms"/>Some days sobriety is just... there. <break time="300ms"/>That's progress too.
<break time="350ms"/>The quiet days count. <break time="300ms"/>Living your life without thinking about it constantly. <break time="250ms"/>That's the goal.

hard_milestone: <break time="400ms"/>I know this milestone is complicated. <break time="350ms"/>Pride and grief mixed together. <break time="300ms"/>Both are valid.
<break time="350ms"/>Some anniversaries hurt as much as they heal. <break time="300ms"/>That's okay. <break time="250ms"/>Feel what you feel.
<break time="400ms"/>You can be proud of your sobriety and sad about what led to it. <break time="350ms"/>Both things are true.
bittersweet: <break time="350ms"/>You've come so far. <break time="300ms"/>And you're grieving the years you lost. <break time="250ms"/>That's real.
<break time="400ms"/>The milestone is real. <break time="350ms"/>So is the loss. <break time="300ms"/>You don't have to choose one feeling.
<break time="350ms"/>Recovery gives you your life back. <break time="300ms"/>But you mourn the version that didn't have to recover. <break time="250ms"/>That's human.

looking_ahead: <break time="400ms"/>Every sober day is a choice. <break time="350ms"/>And you're choosing well. <break time="300ms"/>Tomorrow is another opportunity.
<break time="350ms"/>The days add up. <break time="300ms"/>The weeks become months. <break time="250ms"/>The months become years. <break time="200ms"/>One day at a time.
<break time="400ms"/>You're building a life. <break time="350ms"/>Not just avoiding one thing. <break time="300ms"/>Actually building something.
encouragement: <break time="350ms"/>Keep going. <break time="300ms"/>Whatever you're facing next, you've already proven you can handle hard things.
<break time="400ms"/>Recovery isn't a destination. <break time="350ms"/>It's a way of living. <break time="300ms"/>And you're living it.
<break time="350ms"/>I'm here for all of it. <break time="300ms"/>The milestones and the ordinary days. <break time="250ms"/>All of it matters.
